feed.title: Chinese Semiotic Studies
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from Chinese Semiotic Studies (De Gruyter)

entry.title: A semiotics of freedom: “fabulous” freedom and the agential self in narration as dialogic abduction
entry.summary: Authors: Yunhee Lee DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/css-2026-2003 Pages: Page range: 215-231

entry.title: The semiotics of flourishing: unearthing a Daoist phenomenology of dynamic attunement
entry.summary: Authors: Thomas Michael DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/css-2025-0032 Pages: Page range: 233-255

entry.title: Apophasis, cataphasis, and the distinctions between unsignifiables
entry.summary: Authors: Daniel Fried DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/css-2026-2002 Pages: Page range: 257-272

entry.title: Upliving: lessons from Chinese semiotics
entry.summary: Authors: Massimo Leone DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/css-2026-2001 Pages: Page range: 273-287

entry.title: Toward a trans* theorization of Chinesedanmei
entry.summary: Authors: Ling Yang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/css-2026-2004 Pages: Page range: 289-311

entry.title: Surveillance is only a symptom: artificial intelligence and the systematic enforcement of profile-based identity
entry.summary: Authors: Paul J. D’Ambrosio DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/css-2025-0033 Pages: Page range: 313-331

entry.title: Kitsch, fandom, and the rise and fall of authenticity: an existential exploration
entry.summary: Authors: Hans-Georg Moeller DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/css-2026-2005 Pages: Page range: 333-352
